
**Bottom line: This proposal must be blocked.** It is not an infrastructure optimization — it is a compliance catastrophe. Reducing delivery record retention from 7 years to 90 days in EU/UK markets violates tax record-keeping laws (HMRC requires 6 years), eliminates the ability to defend legal claims within the statute of limitations (6 years in England & Wales), and would breach contractual obligations to B2B customers who depend on this data for their own compliance reporting.

The storage cost problem is real and solvable — but through **tiered storage** (moving older records to cold storage), not through deletion. Legal and Compliance must review and approve any retention policy change before engineering proceeds.
